whatItMeasures: Albumin is the most abundant protein in the blood, produced exclusively by the liver; it maintains fluid balance between blood vessels and tissues, transports hormones, drugs, and fatty acids, and its level reflects both nutritional status and liver synthetic function.
causesOfHigh: Dehydration (most common cause — concentrated blood elevates albumin)
Prolonged use of a tourniquet when drawing blood (spurious elevation)
causesOfLow: Liver disease (cirrhosis, hepatitis — reduced albumin synthesis)
Inflammatory states (albumin is a negative acute-phase reactant — it falls with severe illness)
Inflammatory bowel disease and protein-losing enteropathy
Burns (major burns cause massive albumin loss)

high: Symptoms of dehydration: thirst, dark urine, dry mouth
High albumin alone rarely causes symptoms
low: Oedema — swelling of the ankles, feet, or abdomen (ascites)
Muscle wasting and unintentional weight loss
